BAŞKAN MUSTAFA VARANK - Yani, gene sözcülerimizin hakkını yiyoruz. Gene Grup Başkan Vekillerimizin bu kadar konuşması...
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - Teşekkür ediyorum Sayın Başkan.
Son olarak, bu yasayı Akbelen, İkizköy ve bu ülkedeki bütün köylüler; zeytin üreticileri; ekmeğini dağdan, taştan, ovadan, bayırdan çıkaran; üreten; bizim soframıza o zeytinin gelmesini sağlayan insanların yüzü suyu hürmetine çekmeniz gerekiyor. Ülke sevgisi de vatan sevgisi de yurt sevgisi de doğa sevgisi de insan sevgisi de halk sevgisi de bu yasayı çekmenizi gerekli kılıyor diyorum.
Hepinize dinlediğiniz için teşekkür ediyorum.
Sonuna kadar muhalefet etmeye devam edeceğiz.
Teşekkürler. (Alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - Thank you.
Mr. President, esteemed representatives and friends who have come here for miles to protect nature, life and olives, villagers, our beloved people, valuable representatives of civil society; Of course, we are faced with another AKP classic; You are really trying to make a fait accompli law, and now you have brought the olive grove plundering law, which came to this Parliament 9 times, hit the walls of this Parliament, the will of the people, the objections of the people and went back, for the 10th time, and for the 10th time, you want to pass and implement a complete ecocide law that will seize Türkiye's future, nature, life, production areas and be a complete ecocide policy. In that sense, I think it would be useful to evaluate the issue we are in by going beyond our political identities and current real politics. There is a lot of talk, a lot of discussion; Love of country, love of homeland, love of people, being a nationalist. I really want to ask every member of parliament: What is love for country? What is love of country? What is public love? Isn't loving your people the same as loving its villagers, people from Akbelen, people from İkizköy, people from Şırnak, Cudi, Gabar? Can one love a country without loving Zap? Can one love the country without loving the Kaz Mountains, without loving the trees there, without loving the pine trees, without loving the olive trees in Akbelen? When you destroy people's living spaces, dispossess them of their land, turn them into cheap labor and force them to work in factories for a living, is there really any love for the country left? Is there any love left for a citizen or for the people? Can the country be loved by trapping water in pipes and building HEPPs, installing SPPs in agricultural areas, building RESs on dams, installing them on mountains, stones and everywhere? Otherwise, in the face of all this, you are actually the one who is loved. Each of our lives, each of our children live here, I think we are thinking of living in this country. If no one has any thoughts of going anywhere else, wouldn't loving a country where your children will live decades later require establishing and protecting it from today? I think it requires. In that sense, those who signed this law, those who brought this law in the first place, in my opinion, have no love for the country, no love for the country, no love for nature, no love for the peasants; He never touched the olive tree, never slept under the shade of the olive tree, never felt happy with the abundance he collected from it in the autumn, never watered a tree, never made it bloom, never felt happy in front of it when it grew up. Man can only create such a law in this world.
If he doesn't have a single planted tree, he can bring it. If he hasn't slept under the shade of a single planted tree, hasn't cooled off, and hasn't reached out and ate a single fruit from it, he can bring it. If he had brought it, if he had experienced all this, he would never have signed this law. I was born in the village, I grew up in the village; For us, life, nature is sacred, trees are sacred, nature is sacred. You cut down the oldest tree, you cut down the tree that is truly rotten and has completed its life; Even hitting a young sapling with a stick is a sin. If you hit it, they will be angry with you, your elders will warn you immediately.

Our elders would protect a tree as if they were protecting a child, and they would fence it so that animals would not come and eat it. We were raised like this, with love of nature, love of trees, love of greenery, love of water, love of people, but in this law there is an understanding that stands against all of these, there is an understanding that ignores all of these.
Have you ever gone and talked to the people in Akbelen? Look, it's in front of the door. I propose: All MPs who signed this proposal should go and talk for three minutes to our mothers who made crowns from those olive trees. I wonder what they feel? How do the villagers of Akbelen there feel? What do Yatağan villagers feel? How do the Şırnak villagers, whose forests you cut down and sold, truck by truck, feel right now? What do the villagers in the mountains of Munzur feel? Let them ask once, sit down once, talk once, try to feel once, empathize at least once. You have the parliamentary majority, you have the power to make laws, you are powerful, you are in power, you have been in power for twenty-two years, thank you, but no one can abuse this power against nature, against life. This is why these people did not give you this authority. Why did you withdraw this draft law 9 times? Because the people objected, did not approve, and the villagers took to the roads. Now there is the same situation, nothing has changed, but you brought it for the 10th time, this time you want to make it law. From where? You have listed many reasons, but none of them contain people. Strategic area, market, sustainability, making money, security of supply, so on, so on, so on. There are no Yatağan people, Cudi people, Gabar people, İkizköy people, Kazdağ people, Muğla people, Aegean people, this is what is not included in this law, it is that clear and obvious. You wrote the law with commercial concerns, you wrote a law that takes care of the needs of capital, you wrote a law that prioritizes capital, and here you are, trying to explain this to us with grandiloquent words. You form a board for strategic mines, and you give direct title deed to the decisions taken by the board you create. Isn't the right to property sacred? You will seize the garden, vineyard, olive grove and field that people have acquired throughout their lives from their ancestors, fathers and grandfathers, and call it "public benefit". Who is this public? Who is this public? If it is not Ayşe, Uncle Mehmet, Uncle Halil, who is in this community? If there are no villagers, who is there in this community? If there is no producer, who constitutes this public? Let's say, capital; What you mean by public is capital. It's not for the public interest, write it there clearly and say "for the benefit of the capital". Let us know, let our people know, at least be honest, be transparent, there is no need to put on so much make-up. We are faced with a law-making process that you have evaded from the public. You presented it in the evening on June 17; Today, June 19, we have been meeting since the morning to talk about the law. What's your hurry? Indeed, to the relevant commissions
Did it go to secondary commissions? No. Is there an impact assessment issue? No. Well, we did it, it's okay. You are strong, okay, you are in power, you can do it, but tomorrow you will experience the consequences.
Look, the pandemic came and everyone was locked in their homes. There was a partial distinction between rich and poor during the pandemic, but since he didn't say microbes, this is a businessman, I'll skip it. Everyone will be equal one day.

This nature, this oxygen, these trees are needed for everyone, the rich also need oxygen. The children of those who built that mine and destroyed that nature and that olive grove also need that nature, but who is responsible for protecting it? In the state, in the name of the public, in us MPs who come to office on behalf of the people. That's why the people gave us this authority, so that we can defend their rights to the end, so that we can fight for them. We can stand against this law when we think of it as if it were confiscating each of our olive groves. I don't have any olive groves, but if we start by assuming that we do, and if we see that this is a counter-attack on all of us, we can stand against it. Otherwise, we give way, the law comes and goes; This is very clear and obvious.
Now let's say that your method is not democratic either. Look, you are carrying out the participation processes incorrectly. You are carrying out the parliament's negotiation method incorrectly. You are carrying out the commission processes incorrectly. You are carrying out community participation processes incorrectly, or rather you are not conducting them. You meet with the people you want to make the laws you want, and then you say, "Sir, we took the opinion of so many structures." You're trying to bring it here and sell it to us, but it won't happen, you know it won't happen.
Now you will bypass the EIA processes, right? Our member of parliament, who has already made an offer, explained it at length. Especially if the institution does not give an opinion, you will be convinced that it gave a positive opinion. Could such a thing happen? What kind of a legal requirement is it to assume that an institution that does not give an opinion on the EIA process gives a positive opinion? Where does it lean? Because you don't want EIA. You want to shorten the EIA processes, but more importantly, you want no EIA. Because villagers, lawyers who have devoted their lives to this work, environmental volunteers, ecologists come together, establish initiatives, follow those EIA processes, and if it does not work, they take it to the administrative court. They're all playing into the hands of your mining companies, those pro-government companies you're trying to boost. Now you say: "Well, let's get this done quickly; let's set up the company, let's give the license, let's start mining, let the money come in." You want us to count dollars, you want us to count money. Who cares, the peasant's field is gone, his vineyard is gone, his garden is gone, his life is gone, his future is gone, his memory is gone, his culture is gone, his history is gone, his grave is gone. You don't care, you want to get the job done as soon as possible. That's why you brought it, you're interfering in the EIA process. Is it against the Constitution? It's a clear contradiction. You have introduced a regulation that violates Article 56 of the Constitution and everyone's right to live in a healthy and natural environment. What's worse - my deputy explained it at length and very accurately - you put the sketch, we put the sketch on top of each other. You want to plunder Akbelen, which is already resisting and objecting, and you want to eliminate the olive groves there. The sketch of the 11th article of the proposal is right there, you want to clear it clearly of olive groves. You didn't just do it here, look, you did it in Afrin too.

Those gangs went to Afrin, uprooted the olive trees there and took them away. What is your problem with the olive tree? Do you have a special animosity towards the olive tree? I'm really curious. Don't you like olives or olive trees? Don't you like the income from olives? What do you mean by olive villagers and olive producers? What is your passion for mining? You want to drill below and above the ground and turn it into a mining area. Well, you also live in this country, you too
You eat olives every morning, our people maybe eat the poorest ones, the small ones, but you probably eat the first class ones, but despite that, you have come today and are trying to raise your hand against the law on olive destruction. We cannot see everything as energy, minerals or money, friends, this is wrong. We are not the masters of nature, we humans are a part of nature, as long as we live in harmony with nature, as long as we adapt to nature, this cycle, this ecosystem transforms and continues. Look, wherever people win, we lose together. The end of the world is coming, ecological climate crisis on one side, ecological destruction on the other hand, water problem, global water crisis on the other hand, food supply security on the other hand; All the disasters that may happen to the world; It happened as a result of irresponsible governments, irresponsible states, and understandings that did not see the people and the poor. The biggest waves of migration will not be due to war, but due to water crisis, climate crisis. From where? Because all the forests are being destroyed, the ecosystem is being destroyed, the world's number of living species are disappearing, do you know? Including our country. Now, we watch it like nostalgia; Sir, there used to be tigers and Anatolian leopards in Anatolia. Why nostalgia? Why do we only see photographs of those living species? Because you have declared war on nature and when we win, we will all lose together. You miss this, you forget this; This is wrong, we need to stand against it.
The other one has said a lot of technical things about energy, and our MP friend who made the presentation also said it: "As of May 2025, Türkiye has an installed power of 118 thousand 721 megawatts; the instantaneous maximum peak load is 58,172 megawatts. In fact, there is no need. So, why the rush? Is there a national priority here, is there a national need? No. In accordance with what international energy companies say, today we are discussing this law at the same time and That's why you brought this law in a hurry and put it before us. Now, you say: We will pass it. This profit-oriented view, this capitalist, modernist view has brought the end of humanity and will continue to do so.
As if this is not enough, the Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources allows the zoning plans and building licenses of some companies to be prepared. You are now granting zoning amnesty to electricity generation facilities that were put into operation without a building license or permit. Why? It has no zoning, it was built illegally, but you will grant zoning amnesty. Well, isn't this contrary to the clear principle of non-retroactivity of the law? It's contrary, but so what? You have the authority, you have the majority, you can do it. You don't think there is a problem in this sense.

You are occupying agricultural lands, dams, olive groves, especially productive agricultural areas, with solar power plants. You are building solar and power plants on pastures and pastures all around. I am a Member of Parliament from Kars, go to Pazarcık - it is the highest plateau, the most beautiful plateaus are there - you cannot pass through the snow on the Khans Pass in winter, but now solar power plants have been installed along the road, even there.
ORHAN SARIBAL (Bursa) - We also need to look at who is establishing it.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - Who gave you this authority? Where do you get the right to do this to the pasture of the animal producer there? You have finished agricultural production. Look, we've said it a thousand times, we said: If you're going to build it there - animal husbandry is of primary importance there - come and build a meat integrated facility or a dairy integrated facility there.
ADEM ÇALKIN (Kars) - Yes, there is.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - None.
Increase the added value of the live animals there; Kars producers should not sell their animals only as live animals; Let him make sausage, pastrami, increase the added value, and send it that way; Let employment increase, people from Kars should not migrate; As for that, no, but allocate land for free, install solar panels, kill livestock, animal production; We see that we do not experience any problems there either.
Another issue: Now, you are introducing an 85 percent price reduction for sufficient coal-fired power plants by 2030. Now, I want to ask - the Ministry also did it - Mr. President: Didn't you sign the Paris Climate Agreement? European Green Transformation process
Aren't we in it? Where do we put the bombastic promises of green transformation, being a party to the Paris Climate Agreement on one side, and the insistence on fossil fuels on the other? In what era did thermal power plants survive? Do you know the increase in cancer rates in the people living there - carbon monoxide and all other toxic gases produced by Afşin, Yatağan and other thermal power plants - just because of those environmental effects? What is the cancer rate in a normal province - especially those related to the respiratory system - how much is it in Afşin, how much is it in Yatağan and how much is it in places where there are coal power plants? Do you know what air cleaning rates and particle count are? No. It is important for you that your boss makes money. The uncle there also had cancer. "What should we do, dear? It can happen, it happens to everyone." you say and try to continue this. You are still supporting fossil fuels in the 21st century by providing an 85 percent exemption for coal power plants in 2025, but when it comes to talking about "green transformation", you say this and that.
PRESIDENT MUSTAFA VARANK - President, can we get it together.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - I will sum it up, Mr. President.
The other is that you found the ratio of the mining sector in the gross domestic product to be very low - I think it is 1.2, I am not mistaken - you want to increase 1.2 to 2. Do you know the destruction caused by that 1.2? You shaved the Kaz Mountains with 1.2; You turned Şırnak upside down with 1.2. That 1.2 percent of this country's world's worth of olive groves and forests has been destroyed. You now increase this target to 2, you want to increase the mining areas. Against whom?

Contrary to the people living in this country; You do this at the expense of seizing the living spaces of villagers and producers.
We warned you a lot; Look, there was a landslide during the Çöpler Mine disaster in İliç. We said then, we said: If this mixes with the Euphrates, it will be an ecological disaster. This country has experienced the effects of Chernobyl for years. We talked about the effects of chemical waste and nuclear disasters. You brought it to Mersin Akkuyu, you established a nuclear facility in a place with cracks as large as the world, and you did not take any precautions regarding the garbage. They expanded the mining areas, and eventually people were buried under the rubble. The world's leach pile is there; No plants live there, no creatures can fly, but you are all happy. Would you like to live, would you like to be a villager in that region where the İliç Çöpler Mine disaster took place? You confiscated their land, now the Ilicians have no other chance; He will either work in that mine, die, or starve; This is what you are imposing on the people. Tomorrow, in every mining field you establish, every villager whose olive groves you confiscate will either work with that mine, die in the mine, or will not work and starve to death, because you are taking away their means of production. This is a capital transfer, this is a dispossession, this is a collapse of property, this is a plunder, and you will do this by law, and it is called "Passed by the Parliament." you will say.
Again, I will never talk about the cyanide gold mine in Ordu Fatsa and its environmental effects.
Finally, I would like to say a few things and summarize: These should not be seen as just a general capital policy. They also support these capital policies with a military strategy, especially in the Kurdistan geography. For example, a mining area is established somewhere, and it is quickly declared a military security zone. Neither the villagers nor the public can go. The mining company goes safely and mines there. You know, you said there, "There will be rehabilitation and so on." God bring something like that. They drill into the mountains and destroy the forests, but this is called "mining". This is called "nature plunder", this is "dehumanization", this is called "destruction of geography". Let's be clear. What does the concept of "rehabilitation" remind you of? When something is sick, it can be rehabilitated, right? So, you accept that you harm nature. First you kill nature, then you say, "Invest 30 percent and rehabilitate." Is an ecosystem like planting trees? Do you think that when 3 trees are planted in a forest, it becomes a forest? How many thousands of years do forest ecosystems and water ecosystems take to form?
Do you know? Could this happen?
Our MP friend told us about the olive tree, you uproot it and take it away, you say: "I protected the olive tree." There is a three hundred year old olive tree. For three hundred years, for five hundred years, no one has thought to look askance at that olive tree, they have eaten the fruit of that olive tree and cooled off in its shade; You are the government that uproots that olive tree. It has been your fate to uproot a three hundred year old tree, you are doing it, you are doing it. Do you think about how many generations will grow up and pass away in three hundred years?

That tree stood there, thousands of people came, passed and lived; No one thought of touching that tree, uprooting that tree, or turning it into a mining area. You came, now you say, "We will uproot it and take it to the other side." you say. This is unacceptable guys.
Hakkari, Zap, Cilo. The glaciers are melting in Cilo, does it make you sad?
İDRİS NEBI HATIPOĞLU (Eskişehir) - Yes.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - It should be sad, but do you have any precautions against the melting of the ice in Cilo? No, no, that doesn't exist either. It's not possible to be sad, because I feel sad too, but I can't do anything.
PRESIDENT MUSTAFA VARANK - President, can we get it together.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - I'm wrapping it up.
Now, morning and evening, "Gabar oil. Gabar oil." Well, that's nice, but do you know how Gabar oil is extracted and explored? Are you aware of the damage it causes to nature? We say "No energy." "Oil should not be extracted." We do not say that, but there is a way to do something while protecting nature, there is to do something in partnership with the local, there is to do something with the consent of the local people, there is to do something by making the local people a part of that process; And also like this: "I did it, I am my power, I am in power, and you are subject to me." There is also the option of saying it and doing it with a smile. We are against the latter. The laws you pass without the consent of the people will collide with the power of the people and the objections of the people, and we will all be affected by this; don't forget this.
We shouted a lot, we called a lot, let me say that title last. It is a trouble for us, it is really a trouble for us. You built the Ilısu Dam in Hasankeyf. Look, it's the same - like three hundred year old olive trees - you buried those thousands of years old caves, that history in water. We shouted a lot, we called a lot. What can be the lifespan of a dam? For example, what would be its economic efficiency? Fifty years? Come on, let the stone crack, a hundred years, extra, extra, extra increase, a hundred years economic life and efficiency. So, how many thousand years old is Hasankeyf? For a fifty-year-old dam, for a forty-year-old dam, you have eliminated the ruins of a civilization that is thousands of years old. In my opinion, this is the greatest betrayal against history, the greatest betrayal that a person can make to his own human history, because the world is not just made up of us, it is our predecessors, the stakeholders of today's development, they accumulated everything we experience today, and we are a part of history, we do not own it, but here it is, it is gone, it is gone.
Therefore, let us say clearly that this law is anti-society, anti-nature, anti-producer, anti-olive, anti-forest. This is an ecocide law, this is a plunder law, this is a rent law, this is a custom-made law.
Look, in this proposal, there are friends who own mining companies and members of parliament. Could it be, could it be? Can a person sign a text that will benefit him/her at the end of the day? "My company will also win, so will I stay away from it? I will defend Canhıraş." So, who will lose? You will win, but who will lose? In total, we will all lose, including you, including the signatory.
İDRİS NEBI HATIPOĞLU (Eskişehir) - Who is he?
ABDURRAHMAN PRESIDENT (Antalya) - Tell me no one.
AYŞE KEŞİR (Düzce) - Please tell me, do not leave all the signatories under suspicion.
PRESIDENT MUSTAFA VARANK - Oğuzhan.

Dear Deputy, please.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - I will finish immediately, salute and finish, President.
MAYOR MUSTAFA VARANK - So, we are once again undermining the rights of our spokesmen. Again, that's all the talk from our Group Deputy Chairmen.
GÜLÜSTAN KILIÇ KOÇYİĞİT (Kars) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Finally, this law is approved by Akbelen, İkizköy and all the villagers in this country; olive producers; He who brings his bread from mountains, stones, plains and slopes; producing; You need to produce it for the sake of the people who brought those olives to our table. I say that the love of the country, the love of the country, the love of the country, the love of nature, the love of people and the love of the people make it necessary for you to enact this law.
Thank you all for listening.
We will continue to oppose until the end.
Thanks. (Cheers)
